blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I went downstairs and told my mom I felt weird. I tried to make it sound small. I didn't want her to panic. I said 'weird' when I meant 'I'm scared.'

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: She took one look at my face and she didn't argue. She didn't ask questions. She just picked up her keys.
The gondola rocks gently as the wheel loads another car below them.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: We went to urgent care first. They did an EKG. I remember the stickers they put on me — the little adhesive ones — and how cold the gel was and how the nurse had a very practiced face that didn't tell me anything. They said the results were abnormal and they were referring us.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I remember my mom saying 'okay' in this voice that was completely level. She waited until I was looking away before she pressed her hand over her mouth.
Cassandra's own hands are folded in her lap. She watches them as she speaks.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I saw her do it. I didn't say anything. We drove home and I put myself to bed and I laid there in the dark for a long time, and that was the last night I ever slept without knowing something was coming.
